title: Self-Consistency Improves Chain of Thought Reasoning in Language Models

defines.name: self-consistency
defines.definition: A decoding strategy for chain-of-thought prompting that samples multiple reasoning paths and selects the most consistent final answer by marginalizing over the sampled paths.

problem: Greedy decoding in chain-of-thought prompting can be repetitive, locally optimal, and overly dependent on a single reasoning path.
